title: Zidong Li
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Zidong Li
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
published_college: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

organizational_affiliations.label: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

biography: I joined Christopher Newport University in Fall 2022. As an exercise physiologist, I strive to promote the health benefits of physical activity for everyone. In addition, caring about the health and performance in athletes, firefighters, military personnel, etc., I study potential adverse effects of exercise in the scenarios where the exercise is prolonged, performed in the heat, and induces muscle damage. I am originally from China and have been in the US since I was in graduate school. I am a big sports fan and enjoy watching and playing soccer, tennis and pickleball.
education_entries: Ph D in Exercise Physiology, University of New Mexico
MS in Exercise Science, Southern Illinois University Carbondale
BE in Sports Science, Beijing Sport University
research_interests: Muscle physiology, exercise in the heat and hypoxia, interventions on exercise performance and recovery.
teaching_interests: Exercise physiology, exercise testing and prescription, sports nutrition.
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2025). Acute sodium bicarbonate supplementation reduces the increase in markers of acute kidney injury during physical work in the heat . European Journal of Applied Physiology.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2024). Strategies to Mitigate Acute Kidney Injury Risk during Physical Work in Hot Environments . American Journal of Physiology-Renal Physiology. Volume, 326. Issue, 3. Pages, F499-F510.

Conference Proceeding (2023). Does a Caffeinated Fitness Drink Affect Performance and Physiological Responses during a VO2max Test? – A Pilot Study . JOURNAL OF THE INTERNATIONAL SOCIETY OF SPORTS NUTRITION. Volume, 20. Issue, S2. Pages, 34-35.
Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2022). Interventions to Prevent Exercise-Induced Gastrointestinal Permeability through HSP Induction . Scientific Scholar. Volume, 26. Pages, 43-51.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2022). The effect of prolonged interval and continuous exercise in the heat on circulatory markers of intestinal barrier integrity. . European Journal of Applied Physiology. Volume, 122. Issue, 12. Pages, 2651-2659.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2022). The combined effects of exercise-induced muscle damage and heat stress on acute kidney stress and heat strain during subsequent endurance exercise. . European journal of applied physiology. Volume, 122. Issue, 5. Pages, 1239-1248.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2021). The Potential Role of Exercise-Induced Muscle Damage in Exertional Heat Stroke. . Sports medicine (Auckland, N.Z.). Volume, 51. Issue, 5. Pages, 863-872.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2021). Autophagy in peripheral blood mononuclear cells is associated with body fat percentage. . Archives of physiology and biochemistry. Pages, 1-7.

Presentations: Zidong Li, Sean Gamache, Alex Olmos, Mya Lee, Rachel Singleton, American College of Sports Medicine National Conference, "The Effect of Pre-exercise Feeding on Circulatory Markers of Gastrointestinal Integrity Following Acute Resistance Exercise.," Salt Lake City, UT. (2026)

Lydia Elkins, Caitlin Crump, Zidong Li, American College of Sports Medicine Annual Meeting 2026, "Acute Effects Of Yohimbine HCl Supplementation On Resting Metabolic Rate And Energy Metabolism During Submax Aerobic Exercise," Salt Lake City, UT. (2026)

Abdulaziz Masoud, Abdullah Alansare, Zidong Li, Michael Deyhle, Christine Mermier, Fabiano Amorim, American College of Sports Medicine Annual Meeting 2025, "Blood Pressure Responses to Sodium Bicarbonate Ingestion Prior to Prolonged Exercise in the Heat," Atlanta, GA. (2025)

Abdulaziz Masoud, Zidong Li, Michael Deyhle, Jason Siegler, Zachary McKenna, Christine Mermier, Fabiano Amorim, American College of Sports Medicine Annual Meeting 2024, "The Effect Of Sodium Bicarbonate Ingestion On Acute Kidney Injury After Exercise In The Heat," Boston, MA. (2024)

Rachel Chenoweth, Zidong Li, International Society of Sports Nutrition Annual Meeting, "Does a Caffeinated Fitness Drink Affect Performance and Physiological Responses during a VO2max Test? – A Pilot Study," Ft. Lauderdale, FL. (2023)
